message:
fix(ft-api): multilabel_soft_margin_loss delegates to bce_with_logits_loss [bd-4liu]

multilabel_soft_margin_loss used to extract values, sum the
per-element BCE-with-logits formula in plain f64, and rebuild a
requires_grad=false leaf — silently severing autograd for
multi-label classification training paths.

For a 2-D input [batch, classes] the formula reduces to mean over
all (i, j) of bce_with_logits(x[i,j], t[i,j]), which is exactly
what bce_with_logits_loss computes via composition through
tensor_relu + tensor_abs + tensor_log1p + tensor_mean (all
autograd-aware). Same numerical-stability log1p(exp(-|x|)) form,
same scalar result, but with the autograd tape preserved.

Adds multilabel_soft_margin_loss_propagates_gradient_via_bce_with_logits
regression test using logits = [1.0, -1.0], targets = [1.0, 0.0]
(picked away from the relu/abs corner at 0 so the smooth
sigmoid(x) - t derivative is unambiguous).

Sister of frankentorch-ilwr (cross_entropy with smoothing
severance).

Closes frankentorch-4liu. ft-api lib tests: 1152 passing (+1).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -24,6 +24,7 @@
 {"id":"frankentorch-4ak","title":"Add basic quantization support (torch.quantization equivalent)","description":"PyTorch's quantization allows running models with INT8 weights for faster inference and smaller model size. This is increasingly important for edge deployment.

Implementation plan (CPU quantization only):
1. Add quantized dtypes to ft-core: QInt8, QUInt8 (with scale and zero_point metadata)
2. Add QuantizedTensorMeta extending TensorMeta with quantization parameters
3. Add quantize/dequantize functions:
   - session.quantize_per_tensor(input, scale, zero_point, dtype) -> quantized tensor
   - session.dequantize(quantized) -> float tensor
4. Add quantized linear layer (QuantizedLinear) in ft-nn:
   - Stores INT8 weights + F32 bias
   - Forward: dequantize weights -> matmul -> add bias (or fused quantized matmul if we add a kernel)
5. Add basic observer (MinMaxObserver) for calibration:
   - Tracks running min/max of tensor values
   - Computes optimal scale/zero_point
6. Add post-training quantization workflow:
   - Calibrate observers on representative data
   - Convert float model to quantized model

Tests:
- Unit test: quantize then dequantize recovers original values (within quantization error)
- Unit test: quantized linear forward matches dequantize-then-matmul
- Unit test: MinMaxObserver correctly tracks min/max
- Unit test: scale/zero_point calculation is correct
- E2E test: train a float model, quantize it, verify inference output is close to float model
- Edge case: quantize with scale=0 should error, not divide by zero","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:18:38.229580914Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-28T02:17:51.137107575Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T02:17:51.134684781Z","close_reason":"Implemented MVP fake-quantization slice: tensor_quantize_per_tensor / tensor_dequantize_per_tensor / tensor_fake_quantize_per_tensor on FrankenTorchSession (composes round + clamp + scalar shift; STE backward via tensor_round); MinMaxObserver and QParams with qint8/quint8 helpers in ft-nn; calibrated observer -> qparams -> fake_quantize E2E test. Deferred scope (QInt8/QUInt8 storage dtypes, QuantizedLinear, per-channel quantization) tracked in follow-up bead.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-4do","title":"Implement tensor_histc operation","description":"Add histc (histogram computation) across the stack. Computes histogram of a tensor. PyTorch signature: torch.histc(input, bins=100, min=0, max=0). Used for data analysis and feature statistics.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-27T05:43:19.820291140Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T05:48:53.098223295Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T05:48:53.097979844Z","close_reason":"Implemented tensor_histc with auto-range, clamping, edge cases. 4 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-4i2","title":"Add tests for ft-data sampler builder methods and edge cases","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T01:15:36.117761053Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T01:31:55.127265182Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T01:31:55.127017813Z","close_reason":"Added 18 tests, fixed infinite loop bug in BatchSampler::batches() when batch_size=0","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-4liu","title":"multilabel_soft_margin_loss severs autograd; delegate to bce_with_logits_loss","description":"multilabel_soft_margin_loss at lib.rs:10507 implements the per-element BCE-with-logits formula manually (max(x,0) - x*t + log1p(exp(-|x|))), accumulates in plain f64, and rebuilds a requires_grad=false leaf — same severed-autograd pattern fixed across the recent ft-api cleanup.

For a 2-D input [batch, classes] the multilabel_soft_margin_loss formula reduces to mean over all (i, j) of BCE_with_logits(x[i,j], t[i,j]), which is exactly what bce_with_logits_loss computes (the latter is autograd-aware via composition through tensor_relu + tensor_abs + tensor_log1p + tensor_mean).

Fix: delegate to self.bce_with_logits_loss(input, target) with the existing 2-D shape validation. Same arithmetic, same numerical stability via log1p(exp(-|x|)), but with the autograd tape preserved.

Sister of frankentorch-ilwr (cross_entropy with smoothing severance). Surfaced during /reality-check pass.","notes":"Fixed in this commit. Replaced the manual per-element BCE accumulator with delegation to bce_with_logits_loss, which already implements the same numerically-stable formula max(x, 0) - x*t + log1p(exp(-|x|)) via composition through tensor_relu + tensor_abs + tensor_log1p + tensor_mean (all autograd-aware). For shape [batch, classes] both losses produce identical scalars (mean over all elements) and now both share gradient flow.

Added multilabel_soft_margin_loss_propagates_gradient_via_bce_with_logits regression test. Tests at x = ±1 to avoid the relu/abs corner at x=0 where FrankenTorch's sub-gradient convention differs slightly from the smooth sigmoid(x)-t form. ft-api lib tests: 1152.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T21:16:38.097492106Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T21:19:29.385454393Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T21:19:29.382987561Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Tighten the bridge so unsupported integer/bool dtypes fail closed with explicit diagnostics, add regression tests, and verify via rch.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-06T01:14:09.382741667Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T01:18:44.166077145Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T01:18:44.165821481Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-56r","title":"Implement scaled_dot_product_attention, baddbmm, addbmm","description":"Add F.scaled_dot_product_attention (PyTorch 2.0+ attention primitive), baddbmm (batch add + batch matrix multiply), addbmm (add + batch matrix multiply). SDPA is the core building block for all modern transformer architectures. baddbmm is used internally by attention implementations.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-28T05:34:57.057895284Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-28T05:45:50.487376892Z","closed_at":"2026-03-28T05:45:50.487190188Z","close_reason":"Implemented scaled_dot_product_attention (causal mask, dropout), baddbmm, addbmm. 10 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
